sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The game is over again after clicking on the 9th green block (31, 25). This is consistent with my earlier observation that clicking on ~8-9 green blocks causes the game to reset.
So the pattern is:
- Click on 1 green block: works
- Click on 8 more green blocks: works
- Click on 9th green block: game over
This means I can only click on 8 green blocks before the game resets. But there are 14 green blocks total. So clicking on all green blocks is NOT the solution.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is to click on exactly 8 green blocks (or some other specific number), and then do something else.
Or maybe the goal is to click on green blocks in a specific order, and clicking on the wrong one causes the game to reset.
Looking at the board, there are 14 green blocks in 2 rows of 7. The purple blocks are at the top. Maybe the goal is to click on green blocks that are "aligned" with the purple blocks.
The purple blocks are at cols 14-16, 20-22, 26-28 (centers at 15, 21, 27).
The green blocks are at cols 13, 19, 25, 31, 37, 43, 49 (centers at 15, 21, 27, 33, 39, 45, 51).
So the first 3 green blocks in each row are aligned with the 3 purple blocks. Maybe I need to click on only those 6 green blocks (3 in top row, 3 in bottom row)?
But I've already clicked on 8 green blocks before the game reset, and that included blocks that are NOT aligned with the purple blocks.
Wait, let me re-examine. The green blocks I clicked on were:
1. (25, 13) - col 13, center 15 - aligned with purple at col 15
2. (25, 19) - col 19, center 21 - aligned with purple at col 21
3. (25, 25) - col 25, center 27 - aligned with purple at col 27
4. (25, 31) - col 31, center 33 - NOT aligned
5. (25, 37) - col 37, center 39 - NOT aligned
6. (25, 43) - col 43, center 45 - NOT aligned
7. (25, 49) - col 49, center 51 - NOT aligned
8. (31, 13) - col 13, center 15 - aligned with purple at col 15
9. (31, 19) - col 19, center 21 - aligned with purple at col 21
10. (31, 25) - col 25, center 27 - game over
So the game over happened when I clicked on the 9th green block. But the first 8 clicks were OK. This doesn't seem to be about alignment with purple blocks.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is NOT to click on green blocks at all. Maybe clicking on green blocks is a distraction, and the real goal is something else.
Let me try a completely different approach: move the player to the right edge and see if that clears the level. Or click on the purple blocks. Or click on the sky blue background.
Actually, let me first reset the game with ACTION7, and then try moving the player to the right edge without clicking on any green blocks.
